title: The Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer of Undertakings) Act, 1970
full_text: The Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer of Undertakings) Act, 1970 बैंककारी कम्‍पनी (उपक्रमों का अर्जन और अन्‍तरण) अधिनियम, 1970 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 1970-5 Act Number: 5 Enactment Date: 1970-03-31 Act Year: 1970 Short Title: The Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer of Undertakings) Act, 1970 Hindi Title: बैंककारी कम्‍पनी (उपक्रमों का अर्जन और अन्‍तरण) अधिनियम, 1970 Long Title: An Act to provide for the acquisition and transfer of the undertakings of certain banking companies, having regard to their size, resources, coverage and organisation, in order to control the heights of the economy and to meet progressively, and serve better, the needs of development of the economy in conformity with national policy and objectives and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Finance Department: Department of Financial Services Enforcement Date: 19-07-1969 Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms Schedule 3. THE THIRD SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer of Undertakings) Act, 1970 CHAPTER I PRELIMINAR CHAPTER II TRANSFER OF THE UNDERTAKINGSOF EXISTING BANKS AND SHARE CAPITALS OF THE CORRESPONDING NEW BANKS CHAPTER III PAYMENT OF COMPENSATION CHAPTER IV MANAGEMENT OF CORRESPONDING NEW BANKS CHAPTER V MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title and commencement Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Establishment of corresponding new banks and business thereof. Section 3A. Trust not to be entered on the register. Section 3B. Register of beneficial owners Section 4. Undertaking of existing banks to vest in corresponding new banks Section 5. General effect of vesting. Section 6. Payment of compensation. Section 7. Head office and management. Section 8. Corresponding new banks to be guided by the directions of the Central Government. Section 9. Power of Central Government to make scheme. Show Related Subordinates Section 9A. Power of Reserve Bank to appoint additional director Section 10. Closure of accounts and disposal of profits. Section 10A. Annual general meeting. Section 10B. Transfer of unpaid or unclaimed money. Section 11. Corresponding new bank deemed to be an Indian company Section 12. Removal of Chairman from office Show Related Subordinates Section 12A. Bonus. Section 13. . Obligations as to fidelity and secrecy. Section 14. Custodian to be public servant Section 15. Certain defects not to invalidate acts or proceedings Section 16. Indemnity Section 16A. Arrangement with corresponding new bank on appointment of directors to prevail Section 17. References to existing banks on and from the commencement of this Act.— Section 18. Dissolution Section 18A. Supersession of Board in certain cases Section 19. Power to make regulations. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Amendment of certain enactments Section 21. Repeal and savings Schedule 1.
